subject: Physics
title: The First Photo of a Black Hole
description: Learn The First Photo of a Black Hole in one minute.

hook.text: In 2019, scientists captured the first-ever image of a black hole—55 million light-years away in galaxy M87.

buildup.text: The Event Horizon Telescope linked radio dishes worldwide to create an Earth-sized virtual telescope.

discovery.text: The image showed a glowing ring of superheated gas around a dark shadow—the black hole's silhouette.

twist.text: The black hole is 6.5 billion times the mass of our Sun. Its event horizon is larger than our solar system.

climax.text: The image confirmed Einstein's predictions about how light bends around black holes. General relativity passed another test.

punchline.text: Eight telescopes. One image. Einstein confirmed 100 years later.

quiz.question: How was the first image of a black hole captured?
options: By linking radio telescopes worldwide into one Earth-sized virtual telescope
By sending a probe into the black hole
By using the Hubble Space Telescope
